prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response: I now have the complete picture. Let me synthesize all findings and deliver the diagnosis, fix, and codified rule. --- ## Diagnosis **Two problems, not one.** ### Problem 1 — The playground page *is* already documented (partially) Reading `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` shows the `solid` variant section was added: it has a `ComponentPreview` titled `"Solid"`, the `codeSolid` snippet, and a `PropsTable` entry for `appearance`. Credit where due — this one is actually there. ### Problem 2 — The SCSS module is missing the `.solid` class (the real bug) The component (`Navigation.tsx`) applies `styles.solid` conditionally: ```ts appearance === "solid" && styles.solid, ``` But `Navigation.module.scss` **never defines `.solid`**. The class resolves to `undefined` at runtime. The playground renders `` but it looks **identical to `frosted`** because no CSS rule exists to differentiate it. The variant is documented but broken. **Root cause: EAP-082** — *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page* — and its CSS mirror: the SCSS module was not updated alongside the TSX, so the variant exists in TypeScript-land but has no visual implementation. This is also the condition EAP-102 warns about: `styles.solid` silently resolves to `undefined` with no build error. --- ## Fix ### Part A — Add `.solid` to `Navigation.module.scss` The `frosted` appearance is the implicit default: a translucent `var(--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle)` background. The `solid` variant needs a fully opaque surface with no backdrop blur bleed. Looking at the SCSS, the `::after` pseudo-element is the blur tail — it should be suppressed for `solid`. **`src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss`** — add at the end of the `.nav` block: ```scss .nav { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: var(--portfolio-z-sticky); background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05); &::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 10px; transform: translateY(100%); backdrop-filter: blur(3px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(3px); mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2), transparent); -webkit-mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2), transparent); pointer-events: none; } // ─── Solid variant ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── // Fully opaque surface; no blur bleed below the nav edge. // Use on pages where the nav must be a hard visual boundary (e.g. case // study headers, pages with no scrollable content behind the bar). &.solid { background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05); // opaque counterpart backdrop-filter: none; -webkit-backdrop-filter: none; &::after { display: none; // suppress the blur-tail — it has no meaning on an opaque surface } } } ``` > **Token note:** If `--portfolio-surface-terra-05` doesn't exist in your token set, use the appropriate fully-opaque terra surface token from `src/styles/tokens/`. The key constraint is that it must be opaque (no alpha), in the same terra family as `--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle`. Check `src/styles/tokens/` to confirm the exact name. ### Part B — Verify the playground page is complete (it is, but confirm two things) The playground page at `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` already has the correct structure. Do a quick audit against these three criteria: | Check | Status | Notes | |---|---|---| | `ComponentPreview` for `"frosted"` | ✅ Present | `` | | `ComponentPreview` for `"solid"` | ✅ Present | `` | | `PropsTable` lists `appearance` with both union values | ✅ Present | `'"frosted" \| "solid"'` | No playground page edits needed — it was written correctly. The only missing piece was the SCSS. ### Part C — Flush and restart (mandatory per guardrail #11) Because `Navigation.module.scss` lives in `src/` and is consumed by the playground via `@ds/*`: ```bash # 1. Kill the playground server lsof -ti :4001 | xargs kill -9 # 2. Clear Turbopack cache rm -rf playground/.next # 3. Restart npm run playground & # 4. Wait for 200 curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:4001/components/navigation # 5. Verify the .solid rule is in the compiled output curl -s http://localhost:4001/components/navigation | grep -i "solid" ``` --- ## Codified Rule (the "next time" fix) This is the rule that prevents this from being a per-feedback chase. Three locations need it: ### 1. `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — update EAP-082 The existing EAP-082 says "don't skip the playground page." It needs a second clause: **the SCSS module is part of the component contract and must be updated in the same commit.** Add this to EAP-082's entry in `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md`: ```markdown #### EAP-082: Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page **Trigger:** A new `appearance`, `variant`, `size`, or `state` prop is added to a `src/components/ui/*/` component, but either (a) the playground page has no `ComponentPreview` for it, or (b) the SCSS module has no corresponding class for it. **Why it's wrong:** The component's TypeScript prop exists and the playground may even render ``, but because CSS Modules silently resolve missing class names to `undefined`, the variant is visually identical to the default. The playground looks documented but is lying — it shows nothing. This is a two-surface contract: TSX + SCSS must both ship, and the playground page must verify all of them render distinctly. **Correct alternative:** 1. Add the SCSS rule (`.variantName { ... }`) in `*.module.scss` **in the same commit** as the TSX prop. 2. Update `playground/src/app/components//page.tsx` with a `ComponentPreview` per variant, a props table row, and a code snippet. 3. Flush-and-restart the playground and visually confirm each variant renders differently from the default. **Component-authoring checklist (run before closing any variant PR):** - [ ] TSX: prop added to type union and conditional class applied - [ ] SCSS: `.className` rule exists for the new variant (grep `styles.` → confirm match in `.module.scss`) - [ ] Playground: `ComponentPreview` exists for the new variant - [ ] Playground: `PropsTable` lists the new value in the `type` column - [ ] Visual: the new variant renders **observably differently** from the default **See:** Guardrail #24. Related: EAP-102 (silent `undefined` from CSS Modules). ``` ### 2. `AGENTS.md` — strengthen Guardrail #24 Find the existing `guardrail-engineering-24` anchor and replace its text: ```markdown 24. **ALWAYS** update the playground page **and** the SCSS module when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component. This is a two-surface contract: (1) `*.module.scss` must define the CSS class the TSX conditionally applies; (2) `playground/src/app/components/*/page.tsx` must have a `ComponentPreview` per variant, a props table row, and a code snippet. Missing either surface means the variant exists in TypeScript but is visually silent — CSS Modules resolve missing class names to `undefined` with no build error (EAP-102). Verify by grepping `styles.` in `.module.scss` and visually confirming the playground renders it distinctly. See EAP-082. ``` ### 3. `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` — add a variant-authoring gate Append this section before the closing `## Non-Negotiable` block: ```markdown ## Variant-Authoring Gate (runs when adding any new prop value or variant) When you add a new `appearance`, `variant`, `size`, or `state` value to any `src/components/ui/*/` component, run this checklist **before** closing the task: | Step | What to check | How to verify | |---|---|---| | SCSS class | `.module.scss` defines a class for every value the TSX applies via `styles.` | `grep 'styles\.' *.tsx` → confirm match exists in `.module.scss` | | Playground preview | One `ComponentPreview` per variant value | Read `playground/src/app/components//page.tsx` | | Props table | `PropsTable` `type` column lists all union members | Same file | | Code snippet | Each variant has a code example | Same file | | Visual distinctness | The variant renders observably differently from the default | Flush-restart → curl → open in browser | **Failing any step = task is not done.** The playground page may say "solid" exists while rendering nothing — CSS Modules silently coerce missing class lookups to `undefined`. (EAP-082, EAP-102, Guardrail #24) ``` --- ## Principle > **A variant is not done when the prop is typed. A variant is done when three things exist in the same commit: the TypeScript prop, the SCSS rule, and the playground preview. Any one of the three alone is a lie.** The pattern that makes this a recurring chase is that "adding a variant" feels complete after the TSX change because TypeScript compiles, the prop autocompletes, and no error fires. CSS Modules' silent `undefined` coercion (EAP-102) means the missing SCSS is invisible until a human looks at the screen. The fix is to treat the checklist in EAP-082 as a pre-close gate, not a post-feedback cleanup — the same way the flush-and-restart (guardrail #11) is a pre-report gate, not an optional verification step.
